outcome_text: Decision-making related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks throughout the lifecycle is informed by a diverse team (e.g., demographic diversity, disciplinary expertise, domain experience, and formal and informal training).
scoring_text: Skill in building and managing diverse AI risk management teams, including assembling cross-disciplinary expertise, incorporating varied domain experience, ensuring demographic representation, and integrating diverse perspectives into AI risk decision-making processes.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in building and managing diverse AI risk management teams, including assembling cross-disciplinary expertise, incorporating varied domain experience, ensuring demographic representation, and integrating diverse perspectives into AI risk decision-making processes.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-3.1 (GOVERN) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.577, cosine: 0.737). Secondary: RM-A-003 (RM, cosine: 0.677), RM-S-008 (RM, cosine: 0.677).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.577). Shared vocabulary: [ai, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assembling, building, cross, decision, demographic, disciplinary] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.872, R=0.888). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (75.9%). Semantic tension between "Skill in building and managing diverse AI risk management teams, including assem" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.737 > STS 0.577 (gap +0.159). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, risk, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assembling, building, cross, decision, demographic, disciplinary]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 75.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.159 with low STS 0.577. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
